narrative:
Erling Haaland is Norway's leading Centre-Forward. His overall rating of 7.8/10 positions him as a top-tier talent, ranking in the top 1% of centre-forwards at this tournament. This rating is predominantly based on 73% club form from the last two seasons, with 27% from his current World Cup performances.

At this World Cup, Haaland has been exceptionally clinical, scoring 1.667 goals per 90 minutes from 1.284 expected goals per 90. This efficiency is also evident in his club form, where he recorded 0.806 goals per 90 minutes over the last two seasons. While his expected assists per 90 minutes at 0.071 is low, he still contributes 1.333 key passes per 90 minutes.

Haaland undeniably serves as Norway's main attacking spearhead, playing all 270 minutes so far. Scouts should continue to observe his remarkable goal-scoring output and his ability to exceed his expected goals. His high volume of 3.667 shots per 90 minutes, coupled with 1.667 clearances per 90, makes him a player to watch closely in upcoming matches.
